sources: docs/core/SOFTWARE_CANON.md The boundary rule / L2 ownership kernel row (ratified) — pure predicate over witnessed inputs, fail closed
docs/core/DECISIONS.md 40 (ratified) — a name with no recovery descriptor is one key, nothing to monitor; no recovery path

inputs.attackFlagText: Absence of a descriptor is unprovable from served evidence alone — the rule is only sound stated fail-closed (no witnessed descriptor evidence ⇒ reject), never as 'verify none exists'.

fixture.caseNoWitnessedDescriptor: a RecoverOwner whose recoveryDescriptorHash matches NO descriptor in the witnessed evidence set -> reject (ignored, owner unchanged, no pendingRecovery)
fixture.failClosedFraming: the rule is 'no witnessed descriptor evidence => reject', NEVER 'verify none exists' (absence is unprovable from served evidence)
fixture.priorState: a name; the recover event references a descriptor hash not present in the witnessed evidence set

inputs.scopeNote: Verdict authority RATIFIED: SOFTWARE_CANON boundary rule (pure predicate over witnessed inputs, fail-closed) + DECISIONS #40 (recovery opt-in; no descriptor = owner-key-only, no recovery path). Fail-closed on absent witnessed descriptor — never 'verify none exists'. #50-INDEPENDENT: the existence/absence test PRECEDES the signer predicate (which key invokes recovery is the #50 recovery-auth question). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES (B3): the served-evidence witness FORMAT.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: a RecoverOwner referencing a descriptor hash not present in the witnessed evidence set is ignored: owner unchanged, no pendingRecovery; the rule is fail-closed (no witnessed descriptor => reject), never 'verify none exists'
expected.rationale: This flag is purely about the kernel's fail-closed posture on absent evidence: 'no witnessed descriptor evidence => reject', never 'verify none exists'. Per the gate, a flag about fail-closed posture on absent evidence is derivable now even though the served-evidence format is a B3 deliverable. The verdict (RecoverOwner against a name with no witnessed armed descriptor => ignored, no pendingRecovery) derives from ratified SOFTWARE_CANON (pure predicate over witnessed inputs, fail closed) and DECISIONS #40 (no descriptor => owner-key-only, no recovery path). No dependency on #49/#50 (the existence/absence test precedes the signer predicate).

sources: docs/spec/WIRE_FORMAT.md §5 Keys and owner-key Schnorr digests (normative) — authorization semantics are kernel rules (B2); wire defines digests/verification only; 'a signature valid in one context MUST NOT verify in any other'

inputs.attackFlagText: WIRE_FORMAT §5 explicitly routes authorization semantics to B2 — the wire only verifies (sig, key, digest); without this kernel rule any keyholder could arm a descriptor for any name.

fixture.caseNonOwnerSigned: a descriptor self-consistently signed by key K != the name's current owner key -> reject (owner_mismatch; does not arm)
fixture.caseCrossContext: a transfer/recover signature over the overlapping fields presented as a descriptor signature -> reject (domain separation: a signature valid in one context MUST NOT verify in another)
fixture.positiveControl: a descriptor whose 64-byte Schnorr sig verifies over the §8.2 digest under an ownerPubkey equal to the current owner key -> arms
fixture.priorState: a name with a known currentOwnerKey

inputs.scopeNote: Verdict authority NORMATIVE WIRE §5: the domain-separation rule ('a signature valid in one context MUST NOT verify in any other') is verdict-bearing (same family as V3/X2, both confirmed normative), and §5 routes authorization semantics to B2 while §8.2 fixes the descriptor digest. The B2 rule R2 binds arming to the CURRENT OWNER key. ARMING-SIDE ONLY (the owner arms by signing the descriptor) — explicitly NOT the invoke signer: #50 recovery-auth governs WHO INVOKES recovery (fresh recovery-key vs BIP322 cold-custody), which is #50-provisional and NOT touched here. Tier normative.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: a descriptor arms recovery only if its 64-byte Schnorr signature verifies over the §8.2 digest under the descriptor's ownerPubkey AND that ownerPubkey equals the name's current owner key; a descriptor signed by any other key, or a cross-context signature, MUST NOT arm
expected.rationale: The arming predicate — a descriptor arms only if its 64-byte Schnorr sig verifies over the §8.2 digest under the descriptor's ownerPubkey AND that ownerPubkey equals the name's current owner key — derives from normative WIRE_FORMAT §5 (which explicitly routes authorization semantics to B2 and defines the digest/verification primitives, incl. the cross-context domain-separation rule) plus §8.2 (descriptor digest). Verdict is 'cited'. This is arming-side, not the invoke signer (#50), so no provisional dependency. A second implementer can derive 'descriptor signed by a non-owner key MUST NOT arm' from the normative routing/domain-separation sentence and §8.2 digest definition.

sources: docs/core/SOFTWARE_CANON.md L2 ownership kernel row (ratified) — pure deterministic predicates; no clock; identical inputs => identical verdict
docs/core/SOFTWARE_CANON.md The boundary rule (ratified) — pure predicate over witnessed inputs
docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md R18 Attack flag — completion must be deterministic over chain height, not refresh-invocation time

inputs.attackFlagText: Legacy completion happens in a derived-state refresh keyed on a caller-supplied currentHeight, not on event application — if the new kernel keeps that shape, completion must still be a deterministic function of chain height with no dependence on when the refresh is invoked.
inputs.predicateSignature: recoveryCompletion(priorKernelState{pendingRecovery}, witnessedChainFacts{height}) -> {verdict, state}  [function of (chain height, prior state) only]

fixture.caseReplayDeterminism: replaying the same blocks in one pass vs incrementally yields byte-identical post-completion state
fixture.caseNoRefreshTiming: the completion verdict is a function of (chain height, prior state) only, not of refresh-invocation timing; a caller-supplied currentHeight cannot make completion non-deterministic
fixture.priorState: a pendingRecovery whose challenge window has elapsed

inputs.scopeNote: Verdict authority RATIFIED: SOFTWARE_CANON L2 + boundary rule (pure deterministic predicate over witnessed inputs incl. chain facts; no clock; identical inputs => identical verdict). Tests ONLY the determinism property (completion = f(chain height, prior state), not refresh-invocation timing). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES (R18 is candidate-stays for this): the precise completion-TRANSITION mechanics (new interval ref = request txid, prior-interval chain death per R4) — those are R18's separate needed-spec-work. #50-independent.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: replaying the same blocks one-pass vs incrementally yields byte-identical post-completion state; the completion verdict is a function of (chain height, prior state) only, never of refresh-invocation timing
expected.rationale: This flag is the determinism requirement: completion must be a deterministic function of chain height with no dependence on when the derived-state refresh is invoked (legacy keys it on a caller-supplied currentHeight). That purity/determinism verdict derives from ratified SOFTWARE_CANON (kernel = pure deterministic predicate over witnessed inputs incl. chain facts; no clock; identical inputs => identical verdict). It does not depend on the precise completion-transition spec gap (new interval ref etc., which is R18's separate needed-spec work) — the determinism property is testable now (replay same blocks in one pass vs incrementally => identical post-completion state). No #49/#50 dependency. Per the gate, determinism/purity posture is derivable now even where adjacent format work is deferred.
